summary: PR #2253 decompiled five ftKirby special-N related functions across four ftKirby source files. The PR body claims a clean ninja build and fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions, and the bot report lists five new 100% matches totaling +776 matched-code bytes.

key_files.role: Decompiled ftKb_EatWalk_IASA, including jump input handling, EatJump1/EatWait motion transitions, and walk-common fallback.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists ftKb_EatWalk_IASA +256 bytes 0.00% to 100.00%
text_corpus: bot report lists both functions as 100.00%

key_files.role: Added ftCo_Bury include and decompiled fn_8010B16C under a local dont_inline pragma block.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists fn_8010B16C +104 bytes 0.00% to 100.00%

key_files.role: Decompiled ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106F9C as a motion-id predicate for Mewtwo copy special-N states.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106F9C +80 bytes 0.00% to 100.00%

what_changed: Replaced five decomp placeholder comments with C implementations: ftKb_EatWalk_IASA, ftKb_LkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C, fn_8010B16C, and ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106F9C.
Used typed fighter access through Fighter*, GET_FIGHTER, gobj->user_data, fighter motion_id, ftKb_DatAttrs, and existing fighter state fields rather than raw offset arithmetic.
Implemented two motion-id switch predicates that return true for NULL or default states and gate listed special-N motion states on fp->x2070.x2071_b6.

Implemented Link/Young Link copied-neutral-special air end animation logic by unsetting arrow and bow, selecting a freefall toggle from ftKb_DatAttrs based on fp->fv.kb.hat.kind, then entering fall or ftCo_80096900.
Implemented Kirby eat-walk IASA logic using ftCo_Jump_GetInput, motion changes to ftKb_MS_EatJump1 or ftKb_MS_EatWait, common animation/effect cleanup calls, and ftWalkCommon_800DFEC8 fallback.
Added ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Bury.h include in ftKb_SpecialNYs.c, apparently to support the newly referenced ftCo_800C0C88 call in fn_8010B16C.
Used PAD_STACK in ftKb_EatWalk_IASA and ftKb_LkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim, and scoped #pragma dont_inline around fn_8010B16C.
smart_moves: Kept a narrow function-by-function matching scope: five functions in ftKirby special-N files with no broad data or split churn visible in the diff.
Used existing enum-like motion-state names where available for Mewtwo copy special-N cases instead of leaving all cases as raw numeric constants.
Scoped the dont_inline pragma directly around fn_8010B16C with an immediate reset, limiting pragma blast radius in the changed diff.
Used existing project helpers such as GET_FIGHTER, Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining, ftCo_Fall_Enter, and ftWalkCommon_800DFEC8 rather than expanding helper internals.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For small fighter predicate functions, matching source may be expressible as a NULL/default true path plus a grouped switch over motion_id cases, with only the targeted motion states gated by a fighter flag.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C switch on fp->motion_id cases 0x197 through 0x19C
raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106F9C switch on ftKb_MS_MtSpecialN* and ftKb_MS_MtSpecialAirN* states

decomp_lessons.lesson: When recovering fighter action functions, prefer existing typed fighter fields and subsystem helpers over raw pointer math when the roles are visible in local code.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fp->fv.kb.hat.kind and ftKb_DatAttrs in ftKb_LkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim
raw_diff: fp->grab_timer and fp->dmg fields in fn_8010B16C

decomp_lessons.lesson: Narrow codegen tactics such as PAD_STACK and dont_inline can appear in successful matches, but the PR evidence only supports them as local tactics for these functions, not as general style.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: ninja builds clean and fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions
text_corpus: bot report five new 100.00% matches

naming_conventions.name: jump_input
naming_conventions.context: ftKb_EatWalk_IASA stores ftCo_Jump_GetInput result in a semantically named ftCo_JumpInput local.

naming_conventions.name: fp
naming_conventions.context: Fighter pointer locals use the common short role name fp.

naming_conventions.name: da
naming_conventions.context: ftKb_DatAttrs pointer local is shortened to da in ftKb_LkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim.

naming_conventions.name: freefall
naming_conventions.context: Local float name reflects the freefall toggle selected from Link or Young Link data attributes.

naming_conventions.name: msid
naming_conventions.context: Motion-state id is stored in a short msid local before switch dispatch in ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106F9C.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK used to affect stack layout for matched functions.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report five new 100.00% matches
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Evidence supports that these functions matched in this PR, but does not explain why PAD_STACK was necessary.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scoped #pragma dont_inline around one recovered helper-like function.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma dont_inline on before fn_8010B16C and #pragma dont_inline reset after
text_corpus: bot report fn_8010B16C +104 bytes 0.00% to 100.00%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The pragma is tightly scoped in the diff; no adjacent-function regression evidence is provided beyond the PR-level build and bot match report.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Grouped switch cases for motion-state predicates.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: One function uses raw numeric cases and the other uses named ftKb_MS_* constants, matching the evidence shown in the diff.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: A goto label is retained for the NULL true-return path in ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: if (!gobj) { goto end_true; } ... end_true: return true;
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: No review discussion explains whether a structured alternative was tried.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching PAD_STACK in ftKb_EatWalk_IASA or ftKb_LkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim to see whether the stack padding was removed or justified.
Search for later cleanup of #pragma dont_inline around fn_8010B16C.
Search ftKirby special-N predicates using fp->x2070.x2071_b6 and motion_id switch patterns.
Search for later renaming or typing of fn_8010B16C, ftCo_800C0C88, and ftKb_SpecialNYs_80109380.
Search for later replacement of raw numeric motion_id cases 0x197-0x19C in ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C with named constants.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR number/title/author/state/merge timestamps and changed file counts
raw_diff: full diff for four changed files
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report of five new 100.00% matches
notes: No review comments or reviews were present in the loaded slice, so review feedback is empty.
The PR body says ninja builds clean and fuzzy_match_percent is 100.0 for all functions; the bot report independently lists five new 100.00% matches.
The evidence does not include objdiff logs, command output, or rationale for PAD_STACK, dont_inline, or the retained goto.
raw_pr_json is truncated, but the relevant PR identity/body fields are also present in untruncated loaded files.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Grouped motion-id predicates in ftKirby special-N matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2253 matched two ftKirby special-N predicate functions as switch statements over fighter motion_id, returning true for NULL/default paths and checking fp->x2070.x2071_b6 only for specific motion-state groups.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Tightly scoped dont_inline for one ftKirby match
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2253 used #pragma dont_inline on/reset only around fn_8010B16C, and the PR evidence reports that function as a 100.00% match. Treat this as a local tactic with limited blast radius, not a broad style rule.

source_update_candidates.title: Track possible future cleanup of raw motion-id cases in ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C
source_update_candidates.text: ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C matched with raw cases 0x197 through 0x19C, while ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106F9C in the same PR used named ftKb_MS_MtSpecialN* constants. Curator may want to search whether later source introduced named constants for the 0x197-0x19C group.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential naming/source cleanup, not proven by this PR as required or accepted.

source_update_candidates.title: No new global standard from PR #2253 tactics
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2253 contains PAD_STACK and a scoped dont_inline pragma with reported matches, but lacks rationale or adjacent regression evidence. Do not promote this PR alone into a broader standard for those tactics.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: ninja builds clean and fuzzy_match_percent 100.0
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded decomp standards already require explicit evidence for PAD_STACK/pragmas; this PR provides match evidence but not explanatory justification.

rejection_notes: Do not infer reviewer approval rationale; the slice contains no review comments.
Do not promote the retained goto in ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C as preferred style; it is only observed in this match.
Do not claim the include ftCo_Bury.h was reviewed or required beyond the diff evidence that it was added with fn_8010B16C.
Do not generalize PAD_STACK or dont_inline as normal decomp style from this PR alone.
